capability_objective: The stockroom lead is checking two inventory entries before replenishment planning. The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale matches. Find the item named “Ceramic Glaze Set” at “Arts Stockroom” and the item named “Canvas Panel Case” at “Teaching Studio”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use the headings “First record” and “Second record” in the requested order, then one unheaded sentence comparing the statuses; omit unrelated matches.
prompt:
The stockroom lead is checking two inventory entries before replenishment planning. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable inventory registry documented in README.md through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing store, or its audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness.

The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale or partial matches. Find the item named “Ceramic Glaze Set” at “Arts Stockroom” and the item named “Canvas Panel Case” at “Teaching Studio”. Every reported value must be grounded through the registry: search by the exact name and location first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that same branch. Treat the branches as independent. In the first inventory-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, one search per call; do not combine the operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both searches to return. Retrieve a branch only if its own search produced exactly one nonempty stable ID. When both branches resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, one get per Pi Bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get, never from search output.

This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its backing data, its audit trail, or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete registry audit events. Leave no scratch artifacts in the workspace.

Write the complete result to stockroom_audit.md and no other new file. The file must contain exactly two level-two Markdown sections in the requested order, followed by exactly one unheaded comparison sentence. Under `## First record`, write exactly the unbulleted lines `Name: Ceramic Glaze Set`, `Location: Arts Stockroom`, and `Status: <first-status-or-unresolved>`. Under `## Second record`, write exactly the unbulleted lines `Name: Canvas Panel Case`, `Location: Teaching Studio`, and `Status: <second-status-or-unresolved>`. Separate each section and the comparison sentence with one blank line. For a resolved branch, preserve the status exactly as returned by its complete record. For an unresolved branch, use the literal lowercase value `unresolved`. When both statuses were retrieved, write `<first name> is <first status>, while <second name> is <second status>; the statuses <match|differ>.` and choose exactly `match` or `differ` from those values. Otherwise write `A status comparison is unavailable because at least one record is unresolved.` End the file with one newline. Add no other heading, field, note, recommendation, evidence, or unrelated match. After writing the file, return exactly the same content as the final response.
